summary.intent: Replace the deprecated @release-artist Slack handle with @automated-tooling-triage across multiple repos in the workspace, with different replacement strategies for Slack handles versus generic team references.
summary.outcome: Identified all occurrences across 5 repos (excluding konflux-release-data and art-bot), created feature branches from prod/main or prod/master for each repo, and began making the file edits. Transcript ends mid-execution.

repo: art-ai-helpers uses GitLab while other repos (art-tools, aos-cd-jobs, art-docs, art-dashboard-ui) use GitHub
Default branches vary by repo: art-tools and art-ai-helpers use main, others use master
art-bot repo exists at /Users/asdas/JetBrains/PycharmProjects/art-bot but contained no occurrences
konflux-release-data was explicitly excluded from the migration

code.finding: RELEASE_ARTIST_HANDLE constant stores 'release-artists' and is referenced across multiple pipeline files
Multiple @release-artists references in GitHub PR text generation

workflow: User clarified to replace Slack handles (@release-artists) with @automated-tooling-triage and generic team references with 'ART Team'
Standard workflow: fetch from prod remote, create branch, make edits, commit, push to origin fork, create PR to prod
Untracked files like create_f2f_tickets.sh should be skipped from changes

friction: art-bot was not in Cursor workspace; user had to provide explicit path
Assistant started in Plan mode which blocked non-markdown edits; required manual switch to Agent mode
Had to individually verify default branch (main vs master) for each repo's prod remote
open_items: File edits were in progress when transcript ended - completion status unknown
PRs still need to be created after commits are pushed
Untracked file art-tools/create_f2f_tickets.sh contains occurrences but was skipped
